name: 13.10-create-10-run-name-workflow
description: Create the 10-run-name GitHub Actions workshop workflow from a natural-language specification that introduces run-name.
model: GPT-5.5 (copilot)
---

# Create 10 Run Name Workflow

## Objective

Create `.github/workflows/10-run-name-workflow.yml` if it has been deleted or needs to be rebuilt.

This prompt is intentionally written as a natural-language workflow request rather than a literal YAML template. Use it to demonstrate how a workflow run can be named dynamically in the Actions UI.

## Natural-Language Workflow Specification

Create a GitHub Actions workflow named `10-run-name-workflow` in `.github/workflows/10-run-name-workflow.yml`.

The workflow should introduce `run-name`:

- Set the workflow run name so it includes `10-run-name-workflow`, the triggering actor, and the Git ref name
- Trigger on any push
- Support manual execution from the GitHub Actions UI
- Configure read-only repository contents permission for `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Define one job named `show-run-name` that runs on `ubuntu-latest` with a 5-minute timeout
- Add a step named `explain-run-name` that prints why `run-name` creates clearer workflow entries, then prints the actor and ref name from GitHub contexts
- Check out the repository with `actions/checkout@v4` using a step named `checkout-repository`
- Add a step named `show-latest-commit` that prints the latest commit in one-line format

## Generation Requirements

1. Generate valid GitHub Actions YAML from the natural-language specification above.
2. Preserve the workflow name, dynamic run name, trigger behavior, read-only permission, job name, timeout, step names, runner, and action version.
3. Keep this workflow focused on `run-name`; do not add event filters, job outputs, artifacts, matrices, caches, concurrency, service containers, or deployment behavior.
4. Use GitHub context expressions for the actor and ref name.

## Validation

After creating the workflow, validate `.github/workflows/10-run-name-workflow.yml` with `actionlint` when available.

If validation cannot reach 0 errors, create `.github/workflows/logs/workflow-prompt-errors.md` and include:

- The failing workflow path
- The validation command used
- The full validation error output
- The likely cause
- Recommended fixes

Focused validation command:

```pwsh
$actionlint = Get-Command actionlint -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($actionlint) {
  actionlint .github/workflows/10-run-name-workflow.yml
}
```
